intro: Neofranciella is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as Neofranciella, which is a type of daisy-like herb. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, colds, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Neofranciella include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Neofranciella is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to take advantage of its potential skin-soothing and anti-inflammatory effects.
